channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Negotiations between the U.S. and Iran at a stalemate, Congress votes to end record DHS shutdown, Trump announces new nominee for surgeon general.
item.content:encoded: Negotiations between the U.S. and Iran at a stalemate, Congress votes to end record DHS shutdown, Trump announces new nominee for surgeon general.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Myanmar attempts to rehabilitate image with Suu Kyi move
item.description: State television in Myanmar says detained opposition leader Aung San Suu Kyi has been transferred from prison to house arrest, more than five years after the military coup that removed her from power.
item.content:encoded:
State television in Myanmar says detained opposition leader Aung San Suu Kyi has been transferred from prison to house arrest, more than five years after the military coup that removed her from power.

(Image credit: Myanmar Military True News Information Team)
item.dc:creator: Michael Sullivan

item.title: Man charged with attempted murder after stabbings of Jewish men in London
item.description: A 45-year-old man was charged with attempted murder in the stabbings of two Jewish men in London, the latest in a string of attacks that have sparked fear and anger in Britain's Jewish community.
item.content:encoded:
A 45-year-old man was charged with attempted murder in the stabbings of two Jewish men in London, the latest in a string of attacks that have sparked fear and anger in Britain's Jewish community.

(Image credit: Alastair Grant)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Republicans say they will defer to Trump on Iran war despite arrival of deadline
item.description: Republican lawmakers say they will continue to defer to President Donald Trump, for now, during the fragile ceasefire with Iran.
item.content:encoded:
Republican lawmakers say they will continue to defer to President Donald Trump, for now, during the fragile ceasefire with Iran.

(Image credit: Cliff Owen)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Hegseth faces questions about Iran in first congressional appearance since war began
item.description: Making his first appearance before Congress since the Trump administration went to war against Iran, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth faced withering questioning from skeptical Democrats.
item.content:encoded:
Making his first appearance before Congress since the Trump administration went to war against Iran, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth faced withering questioning from skeptical Democrats.

(Image credit: Rod Lamkey Jr.)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Prosecutors release video of armed man storming correspondents' dinner
item.description: Federal prosecutors released a video Thursday showing the moment authorities say a man armed with guns and knives tried to storm the White House Correspondents' Association dinner and attempt to kill President Donald Trump.
item.content:encoded:
Federal prosecutors released a video Thursday showing the moment authorities say a man armed with guns and knives tried to storm the White House Correspondents' Association dinner and attempt to kill President Donald Trump.

(Image credit: Department of Justice)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: The Venice Biennale jury resigns amid tensions over awards ban, Russian participation
item.description: The international jury of the Venice Biennale resigned Thursday amid tensions over Russia's participation and the panel's decision to bar prizes for countries accused of crimes against humanity.
item.content:encoded:
The international jury of the Venice Biennale resigned Thursday amid tensions over Russia's participation and the panel's decision to bar prizes for countries accused of crimes against humanity.

(Image credit: Antonio Calanni)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Zelenskyy says he's seeking details of Putin's May 9 ceasefire proposal
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy is seeking details of a short-term ceasefire Russia proposed to U.S. President Donald Trump.
item.content:encoded:
Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy is seeking details of a short-term ceasefire Russia proposed to U.S. President Donald Trump.

(Image credit: Petros Karadjias)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Trump gives the go-ahead for a major new Canada-U.S. oil pipeline
item.description: More state and federal approvals are needed for the 3-foot-wide Bridger Pipeline Expansion, which would stretch from the Canadian border with Montana down through eastern Montana and Wyoming, where it would link up with another pipeline.
item.content:encoded:
More state and federal approvals are needed for the 3-foot-wide Bridger Pipeline Expansion, which would stretch from the Canadian border with Montana down through eastern Montana and Wyoming, where it would link up with another pipeline.

(Image credit: Alex Brandon)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Will.i.am wants to future-proof a new generation
item.description: The Black Eyed Peas co-founder turned entrepreneur is now teaching a class on "agentic AI" for Arizona State.
item.content:encoded:
The Black Eyed Peas co-founder turned entrepreneur is now teaching a class on "agentic AI" for Arizona State.

(Image credit: Emily Choi)
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman
